reasoning: Hikaru Nakamura's Elo as of Oct 2025 is ~2816. A 1% increase requires him to gain over 28 points, putting his rating around 2844, which would be significantly above his lifetime peak and near the all-time records. At K=10, a ~2800 player must outscore their expected score by ~2.8 points to gain 28 Elo points, requiring a historic performance (like Caruana's 2014 Sinquefield Cup) over many games. Furthermore: 1) The Wikipedia FIDE rankings article reflects FIDE's official monthly ratings lists. Since lists only update on the 1st of the month, the rating listed on Nov 16 will be identically the same as on Nov 9, resulting in a 0% change. 2) For Dec and Feb, even if a new list is published, Nakamura is largely inactive in classical chess and achieving a +28 point rating swing in 1-3 months is infinitesimally likely. Uncertainty increases slightly across the horizons simply to respect proper forecasting calibration over longer unknown periods.
